━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
📚 DAILY MARKET EDUCATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📖 TERMINOLOGY: OPEN INTEREST EXPLAINED

Open interest measures the total number of active derivative contracts that haven't been settled yet. Think of it as the number of traders still holding positions in futures or options.

Here's what matters: Rising open interest with rising prices shows strong conviction. New money is entering the market. Falling open interest with falling prices means traders are exiting and taking losses.

Real example: In April 2026, Bitcoin futures open interest jumped 35% while price climbed from 68K to 74K. This confirmed genuine buying pressure, not just short covering.

How to use it:
- High open interest means high liquidity and tighter spreads
- Sudden drops in OI during price moves signal position liquidations
- Compare OI across different strike prices to spot support and resistance zones
- Watch for divergences between price and OI for reversal signals

PRO TIP: When price makes new highs but open interest decreases, be cautious. It often means existing longs are taking profits rather than new buyers entering.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🔍 ADVANCED CONCEPT: DARK POOLS

Dark pools are private trading venues where institutional investors execute large orders away from public exchanges. No price transparency until after the trade settles.

Why they exist: A pension fund buying 2 million shares would move the market publicly. Dark pools let them trade without showing their hand. Roughly 15-18% of US equity volume happens in dark pools.

The smart money angle: Dark pool activity often precedes major moves. When you see unusual dark pool prints in a stock trading sideways, institutions are positioning before news breaks.

Key indicators to watch:
- Dark pool print size relative to average daily volume
- Timing of prints during market hours versus after hours
- Repeated prints at similar price levels indicate accumulation
- Premium paid above current market price shows urgency

Real scenario: June 2026 semiconductor stocks showed massive dark pool buying two weeks before the AI infrastructure spending bill passed. Retail saw flat charts while institutions loaded up.

SMART MONEY RULE: When dark pool volume exceeds 25% of total volume for three consecutive days, major players are repositioning. Follow the flow, not the headlines.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

💡 THREE TIPS FOR WEDNESDAY TRADING

TIP 1 - MIDWEEK MOMENTUM CHECK
Wednesday typically shows the truest directional bias of the week. Monday reactions fade and Friday positioning hasn't started yet. Use today to confirm your weekly thesis. If markets reject key levels today, expect follow-through Thursday.

TIP 2 - ECONOMIC DATA FOCUS
June historically brings volatility around employment indicators and central bank positioning. Check your economic calendar before the US session. Trade smaller size on data release days or wait 15 minutes for volatility to settle before entering positions.

TIP 3 - SECTOR ROTATION MATTERS
As we move deeper into Q2 2026, watch sector leadership shifts. Technology led May but energy and materials are showing relative strength. Diversify your watchlist across sectors to catch rotation plays early. The money always moves, your job is following it.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

⚠️ RISK MANAGEMENT: MANAGING PORTFOLIO EXPOSURE

Portfolio exposure measures how much of your capital is actually at risk across all open positions. Many traders focus on individual trade risk but ignore total exposure.

The danger zone: Having five trades with 2% risk each sounds safe until you realize all five are correlated. A market-wide move could trigger all stops simultaneously for a 10% account hit.

Calculate total exposure properly:
- Add up all position sizes as percentage of account
- Identify correlations between your holdings
- Consider market beta during high volatility periods
- Never exceed 6-8% total portfolio exposure on correlated positions

June 2026 reality check: Markets are showing increased correlation across asset classes. When central banks talk, everything moves together. Your tech stock, crypto position, and growth ETF might all be the same bet in disguise.

Practical protection:
- Cap total exposure at 15% of portfolio maximum
- Use uncorrelated assets as hedges
- Reduce position sizes during earnings seasons and FOMC weeks
- Keep 30-40% cash reserve for opportunity and safety

CRITICAL REMINDER: Your edge isn't just finding good trades. It's surviving bad periods with capital intact to trade another day.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Educational purpose only
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
